codeReview.summary: Pass. Reviewing both diffs shows the round-2 blocker is actually resolved. `f762e05` added the real outer-bash -> `agent` -> inner-`ScoopContext` nesting scenario, but also added a detached `coneCounters`/`createScoopManagementTools(...)` block that was never wired to the execution surface under test. `d87670f` removes that dead instrumentation and leaves the real-runtime assertions intact. In the surviving test, the cone fixture is still registry-only, the nested run still succeeds through the real stack, `stdout` must bubble `inner-ok`, both `agent-*` scratch folders must be deleted, and the orchestrator registry/tab snapshots must return to their pre-run state. That is now real signal for the intended VAL-NEST-015 property instead of a tautological counter proof.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission validation contract still describes VAL-NEST-015 in terms of cone-tool call counters staying zero, but the accepted remediation intentionally deletes those counters and relies on real-runtime stdout/registry/scratch assertions instead. Future workers could be steered back toward the dead-instrumentation pattern unless that wording is clarified.

summary: Pass. Commit `d87670f` removes the dead counter instrumentation identified in the round-2 review of `f762e05` and does not introduce a new regression. The remaining assertions in `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-nesting-real-context.test.ts` now carry the signal: they still drive the real outer-bash -> nested-agent -> inner-`ScoopContext` flow, require the bubbled `inner-ok` result, and verify scratch/registry cleanup with only a registry-only cone fixture present, so the prior vacuous VAL-NEST-015 proof is gone and this fix adequately resolves that blocker within review scope.
